events.title: 25 dead in India tunnel disaster as more bodies recovered
events.description: The bodies of the remaining 13 workers trapped inside a collapsed hydropower tunnel in India have been found - as the death toll rises to 25.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 25 dead in India tunnel disaster as more bodies recovered

events.title: Oil prices surge above $100 a barrel for first time since May
events.description: Global oil prices have surged once again as tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate. Brent crude has climbed above $100 a barrel for the first time since May. While much of the focus has been on the strategic Strait of Hormuz, shipping through the Red Sea is also coming under growing threat after Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels said they targeted two Saudi oil tankers. The escalation comes as Donald Trump said he was considering a "massive attack" on Iran, prompting Tehran to warn it would hold accountable any country supporting a US strike. Peter O'Brien reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Oil prices surge above $100 a barrel for first time since May

events.title: Israel's new exhibit exposes years of Hamas terrorist planning before Oct 7 massacre
events.description: A 600-square-meter exhibition features Hamas tunnel replicas, attack vehicles, and documents revealing Hezbollah and Iran's roles in the Oct. 7 assault.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel's new exhibit exposes years of Hamas terrorist planning before Oct 7 massacre

events.title: Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal
events.description: Foreign minister Penny Wong will deliver speech criticising China’s recent launch of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Penny Wong will warn China that Australia and other neighbouring nations will not be bullied by its “provocative” actions, after its involvement in another “destabilising and dangerous” incident this week. The foreign affairs minister – who will deliver the address on Thursday in the Philippines, where the 59th Asean foreign minister’s meeting is being held – will also caution world leaders against placing a toll on the strait of Hormuz after both Iran and the US threatened to do amid the protracted conflict. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal

events.title: Spain declares wildfire emergency, 10,000 evacuated near Madrid
events.description: The Spanish government ⁠has declared a national emergency after a series of wildfires in the Madrid region and in the province of Avila, near the capital, late on Thursday. The measure aims to speed up the deployment of resources to fight the blazes, which have forced some 10,000 people in the region to evacuate. According to Spanish authorities, "not enough has been done" by the government to contain and prevent these fires, which are no longer a temporary emergency. France 24 correspondent Sarah Morris reports the latest from Madrid.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Spain declares wildfire emergency, 10,000 evacuated near Madrid

events.title: Middle East: Oil surges as Gulf tensions escalate
events.description: Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels say they attacked two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea on Thursday, marking their first reported strike on a vessel since announcing a blockade of Saudi-linked shipping through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait earlier this week. The Houthis say the move is retaliation for Saudi Arabia’s blockade on Yemen and a recent strike on Sanaa’s international airport. The escalation adds further pressure to global shipping as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed and Brent crude prices hit their highest level since May. Morgan Ayre and Charlotte Lam explain why the Bab el-Mandeb Strait matters. Morgan Ayre and Charlotte Lam report.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East: Oil surges as Gulf tensions escalate

events.title: World News in Brief: Sexual violence in DR Congo, deaths in Ukraine and Russia, crime in Southeast Asia
events.description: Independent UN human rights experts on Tuesday condemned the M23 armed group’s “reign of terror” over territory it controls in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), calling for an end to the Rwanda-backed group’s sexual torture and violence against civilians.
events.ticker_label: 💥 World News in Brief: Sexual violence in DR Congo, deaths in Ukraine and Russia, crime in Southeast Asia

events.title: University courses covering Israel-Palestine should be audited, Julian Leeser tells royal commission
events.description: Inquiry’s focus this week is on security for the Jewish community, with the MP telling the commissioner his son is afraid to attend synagogues and events Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The shadow education minister, Julian Leeser, has called for universities to undertake an audit of any classes that teach about the Jewish community, Israel or Palestine. Appearing before the royal commission into antisemitism and social cohesion on Friday, Leeser said he had been “very concerned” about antisemitism on university campuses and was not satisfied with how vice-chancellors had addressed the issue after the 7 October 2023 Hamas terrorist attack. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 University courses covering Israel-Palestine should be audited, Julian Leeser tells royal commission

events.title: France battles escalating wildfire crisis
events.description: Following intense wildfires earlier this week, authorities have ordered evacuations across the villages of Lège-Cap-Ferret, with more than 20,000 people already forced to flee. The situation remains critical, as at least 4,800 hectares have been consumed by the flames. Caitlin Brown has more.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ France battles escalating wildfire crisis

events.title: Deadly Afghanistan floods kill at least 26 as aid agencies race to reach survivors
events.description: Flash floods have killed at least 26 people across eastern Afghanistan, including 23 in the mountainous province of Nuristan, where homes, markets and roads were swept away and search teams continued looking for people reported missing.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Deadly Afghanistan floods kill at least 26 as aid agencies race to reach survivors

events.title: Klopp named new head coach of Germany men's football team
events.description: Germany has turned to Jürgen Klopp to lead a new era for the four-time world champions after their disappointing World Cup exit, with hopes of building toward future international tournaments.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Klopp named new head coach of Germany men's football team

events.title: Spain declares an emergency after wildfires force evacuations of 10,000 people
events.description: Tens of thousands of people in Spain and southern France have evacuated their homes while multiple wildfires rage out of control
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Spain declares an emergency after wildfires force evacuations of 10,000 people

events.title: Stranded seafarers remain trapped as Hormuz shipping stalls
events.description: As the Iran-US war continues, the key question remains unanswered about how shipping through the Strait of Hormuz will be managed in future – although the UN has reiterated that charging tolls for safe passage runs contrary to international law.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Stranded seafarers remain trapped as Hormuz shipping stalls

events.description: Palestinians say villagers came under attack by settlers, while the Israeli military says a Palestinian stole a weapon and opened fire on hikers.

events.title: Spain declares national emergency over wildfires
events.description: At least 10,000 people have been evacuated following wildfires in the central region of Madrid.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Spain declares national emergency over wildfires

events.title: At least 11 killed in Ukraine as Moscow and Kyiv continue to trade attacks
events.description: Six people also killed in Ukrainian attack on Russia’s Kirov, and Wildberries warehouses hit again.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 At least 11 killed in Ukraine as Moscow and Kyiv continue to trade attacks

events.title: Weather tracker: More than 900 wildfires rage across Canada
events.description: Fires remain out of control in Northwest Territories and Ontario, while Argentina endures record night-time heat Recent hot, dry weather in Canada has triggered hundreds of wildfires across the country. As of Wednesday, more than 900 were burning, scorching about 3m hectares of land. While fires have broken out in several provinces, the greatest concern is in the Northwest Territories and Ontario , where several remain out of control. Poor air quality has been reported across affected regions, with wildfire smoke carried by winds into the US and even across the Atlantic to northern Europe. The risk of further blazes remains high as North America enters the peak of fire season. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: More than 900 wildfires rage across Canada

events.title: Australian households face prospect of interest rate hike and petrol prices rising above $2 a litre
events.description: Economists’ warning comes as escalating Middle East crisis pushes global crude oil back above $US100 a barrel Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australian households face the prospect of a Reserve Bank interest rate hike and petrol prices above $2 a litre over the coming weeks, economists warn, as the escalating Middle East crisis pushes global crude back above $US100 a barrel. Financial markets now see an even chance the RBA board will deliver a fourth cash rate increase at the next meeting on 11 August. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australian households face prospect of interest rate hike and petrol prices rising above $2 a litre

events.title: Europe wildfires: Many flee by boat as fires spread, merge
events.description: Tens of thousands hopped onto cars and boats to escape southwestern France's wildfires. In the Madrid region, authorities declared a national emergency as at least two fires merged.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Europe wildfires: Many flee by boat as fires spread, merge

events.title: Why Iran's Pickaxe Mountain is a tough target for the US
events.description: Donald Trump has threatened to attack a suspected Iranian nuclear site buried deep under a mountain. Iran's Pickaxe Mountain remains one of the biggest unanswered questions surrounding the country's nuclear program.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Why Iran's Pickaxe Mountain is a tough target for the US

events.title: Fighting wildfires is getting harder — these tips could help
events.description: With Spain declaring a national emergency, France seeking EU assistance and Canada dealing with more than 900 hot spots, wildfires are out of control. How can we stop our forests from burning out of control?
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Fighting wildfires is getting harder — these tips could help

events.title: Wildfires in Europe prompt tens of thousands to evacuate
events.description: Spain has declared a national emergency in the Madrid region. In neighboring France, meanwhile, President Emmanuel Macron has invoked the EU's civil protection mechanism to ask for help.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wildfires in Europe prompt tens of thousands to evacuate

events.title: Wildfires hound Tunisia, Algeria and Morocco
events.description: Deadly forest fires have been raging across Algeria, Morocco and Tunisia for more than a week, as an ongoing heatwave, dry conditions and strong winds fuel the flames and hamper firefighting efforts. Firefighters across the region are struggling to contain the blazes, which have burned vast areas and forced evacuations. Emily Boyle reports.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wildfires hound Tunisia, Algeria and Morocco

events.title: Could this African nation become Trump's next terror target as jihadists close in on power?
events.description: Mali's gold and lithium deposits add a minerals dimension as Trump's team reportedly weighs strikes against al Qaeda-linked terrorists in the Sahel.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Could this African nation become Trump's next terror target as jihadists close in on power?

events.title: #Ukraine targets Russian #ecommerce giant's warehouses in fourth #attack
events.description: #Ukraine struck several warehouses owned by the so-called '#Russian #Amazon' #Wildberries on July 24, which it says supplies the Russian army. A separate attack on a factory killed at least six people, while Russian strikes just outside #Kyiv killed at least ten.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 #Ukraine targets Russian #ecommerce giant's warehouses in fourth #attack

events.title: France: Two major wildfires hit the southwest forcing tourists to evacuate
events.description: Deadly wildfires are sweeping across western Europe, fueled by scorching temperatures and strong winds, as climate change drives fires to spread faster and burn more intensely. The total area scorched in EU countries this year is already the second largest on record. In France, where three heatwaves have hit since May, wildfires in the southwest have forced more than 20,000 people to evacuate. Around 800 firefighters, backed by hundreds of gendarmes and soldiers, are battling the flames about 50 kilometres west of Bordeaux, as the crisis unfolds during the peak summer holiday season. Among those evacuated are hundreds of tourists who had come to spend their holidays in the area. One of them, Dutch tourist Barry Pollack, joined us to tell us more.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ France: Two major wildfires hit the southwest forcing tourists to evacuate

events.title: 'Incredibly dire': Nine months into Gaza ceasefire, conditions 'should have improved by now'
events.description: Alison Sargent is pleased to welcome Shaina Low, Communications Advisor for the Norwegian Refugee Council's Palestine mission. Despite the reduction in large scale hostilities Low argues that Gaza remains trapped in a state of structural emergency, where humanitarian actors are prevented from moving beyond short term relief because of extensive restrictions on aid, construction materials, fuel, and operational access. She asserts that the central issue is no longer simply the scale of destruction, but the inability to reverse it despite the existence of a ceasefire.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Incredibly dire': Nine months into Gaza ceasefire, conditions 'should have improved by now'

events.title: Five killed in occupied West Bank after Israeli settlers attack
events.description: Four Palestinians and one Israeli were killed in a shooting in the occupied West Bank village of Till
events.ticker_label: 💥 Five killed in occupied West Bank after Israeli settlers attack

events.title: Trump threatens a major escalation in Iran as the war nears the end of Month 5
events.description: The U.S. continued airstrikes in Iran while Tehran launched retaliatory attacks on U.S. targets in the Gulf, as President Trump said he was close to deciding whether to launch "a massive attack."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump threatens a major escalation in Iran as the war nears the end of Month 5

events.title: Guterres calls for stepping up diplomatic efforts to end US-Iran conflict
events.description: The United Nations again called for restraint as war ramps up in the Middle East, underscoring the need for dialogue and an end to attacks on “vital infrastructure” across the region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Guterres calls for stepping up diplomatic efforts to end US-Iran conflict

events.title: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions
events.description: Trump’s 50% tariff threat means there will be no ribbon-cutting celebration of Gordie Howe International Bridge A planned joint US-Canada ceremony on Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is no longer expected to take place after Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods. In announcing the tariffs, the president accused Canada of unfairly discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol and dairy products, escalating tensions with one of the United States’ closest allies. Continue reading...

events.title: Gaza faces ongoing food crisis despite ceasefire
events.description: More than nine months into the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas, two-thirds of Gaza's population is still facing crisis levels of food insecurity, according to a new UN report released on Thursday. The report warns that recent gains could quickly be reversed unless humanitarian agencies continue to receive sustained access to the enclave, along with adequate funding and stability. For more on the humanitarian situation, we're joined by Shana Low, Communications Adviser for the Norwegian Refugee Council's Palestine mission, speaking to us from Amman, Jordan.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gaza faces ongoing food crisis despite ceasefire

events.title: India extends internet shutdown amid youth protests
events.description: India has extended a mobile internet shutdown in the capital, New Delhi, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi faces his biggest youth-led challenge since taking office in 2014. Leaders of the youth-led "Cockroach" movement are expected to meet government officials later today in an effort to resolve a standoff over the country's education system. The talks come hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike, amid mounting public anger over exam irregularities and a series of other scandals. Rachael Griffiths reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India extends internet shutdown amid youth protests

events.title: Six candidates vying to become the next UN secretary after Guterres
events.description: Six candidates vying to become the next UN secretary-general presented their visions for the organisation on Thursday, as the United Nations faces a financial and confidence crisis ahead of a selection process that will determine its leadership from January 2027. France 24 International affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani shares further insights on candidates.

events.title: India youth movement agrees to talk but insists protests will continue
events.description: India has extended a mobile internet shutdown in the capital, New Delhi, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi faces his biggest youth-led challenge since taking office in 2014. Leaders of the youth-led "Cockroach" movement are due to meet government officials later today in an effort to resolve a standoff over the country's education system. The talks come hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike, amid growing public anger over a series of exam irregularities and other scandals. France 24 correspondent Sarah Jacob reports from New Delhi.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India youth movement agrees to talk but insists protests will continue

events.title: Canada reportedly scraps joint bridge celebration with US after Trump renews tariff threat
events.description: Canada canceled a joint celebration for the Gordie Howe International Bridge, connecting Detroit to Windsor, Ontario, after President Donald Trump renewed tariff threats.

events.title: Global hunger declines again but progress remains uneven across regions
events.description: Global hunger declined for a third consecutive year in 2025 showing that progress is possible, but gains remain fragile and far too slow to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by the 2030 deadline.

events.title: Multiple injured after floatplane makes emergency landing, catches fire off Washington coast
events.description: A Kenmore Air seaplane carrying 11 people made an emergency landing near Sucia Island on the Washington coast before catching fire, with one person in critical condition.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Multiple injured after floatplane makes emergency landing, catches fire off Washington coast

events.title: UK's Burnham names Trump critic Ed Miliband foreign secretary; ex-Thatcher aide brands pick 'disastrous'
events.description: Ed Miliband’s appointment as the United Kingdom's foreign secretary sparks warnings of tension with President Trump over his past attacks, net-zero policies and foreign affairs.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK's Burnham names Trump critic Ed Miliband foreign secretary; ex-Thatcher aide brands pick 'disastrous'

events.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
events.description: Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability. There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds

events.title: U.S. signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia. And, Nolan Wells' independent autopsy results
events.description: The U.S. has signed a nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia. And, the independent autopsy results of Nolan Wells fuel more questions surrounding his cause of death.

events.title: France: Why is the Southwest so vulnerable to wildfires?
events.description: The Gironde region around Bordeaux is once again battling major wildfires, four years after a devastating blaze swept through the area. As Morgan Ayre explains, a combination of high-risk factors, including vast forests of maritime pine, persistent drought and heavy tourist traffic, has made the region especially vulnerable to fast-moving fires.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ France: Why is the Southwest so vulnerable to wildfires?

events.title: At least 50 people killed and 700,000 displaced by India floods after monsoon rains
events.description: Officials in India say at least 50 people have died and 700,000 have been displaced by flooding after days of monsoon rains
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ At least 50 people killed and 700,000 displaced by India floods after monsoon rains

events.title: Germany appoint Klopp as ex-Liverpool coach sought to lift World Cup blues
events.description: Jurgen Klopp last coached in 2024 at Liverpool but was Red Bull's head of global football, leading Leipzig and New York.

events.title: Julian Leeser says his son no longer feels safe at Jewish events – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australian exports sent to the US will be hit with a 12.5% tariff as the US president imposes levies on more than 80 nations. The Trump administration unveiled the latest tranche of tariffs for countries over their “failure to impose and effectively enforce a prohibition on the importation of goods produced with forced labour”. This, in our view, makes no sense. Forced labour is definitely something which needs to be combated around the world, so we support that objective. We absolutely need to see the strait of Hormuz be open and for freedom of navigation to happen there, for commercial shipping to be allowed through, and we very much call on Iran to allow that to be the case. We need to see a restoration to the normal global fuel supply chain because it’s having a significant impact around the world. Continue reading...

events.title: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike
events.description: Sonam Wangchuk has ended his hunger strike after 26 days, saying the decision follows a "long negotiation" on various conditions and "in view of possible violence in the country."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike

events.title: Iran urged to stop protest-related executions

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran urged to stop protest-related executions

events.title: Security Council LIVE: How to turn preventive diplomacy rhetoric into action
events.description: In the Security Council debate on Thursday on how best to settle disputes between States peacefully, ambassadors provided examples of success and challenges ahead under such themes as effective early warning and how to move from rhetorical support for preventive diplomacy to ensuring more consistent use of its Chapter VI powers under the UN Charter.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: How to turn preventive diplomacy rhetoric into action

events.title: Two Russian men jailed in Angola for terrorism and spying
events.description: The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last year Two Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern African country last year. Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin were sentenced to eight and 11 years in jail respectively. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cultural and business activities. Continue reading...

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Syria ‘not alone’ in locating missing persons, tackling displacement
events.description: Syria is not alone in tackling challenges, including the hundreds of thousands who remain missing during past tumultuous decades, the head of a UN institution said on Thursday as António Guterres departs on his first visit to the country in his current capacity as Secretary-General.

events.title: Nicaragua's parliament plans to eliminate elections to codify President Ortega's declaration
events.description: Nicaragua's congress moves to abolish elections after President Daniel Ortega declared no more elections, drawing condemnation from the international community.

events.title: Canadian government urged to block data-sharing deal between Thomson Reuters and US ICE
events.description: ICE will pay Canadian media giant US$125m to use its investigative database to identify ‘immigration fraud’ A Canadian federal opposition party leader is calling on the Mark Carney government to block the latest data-sharing deal between US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and the Canadian media giant Thomson Reuters. Avi Lewis, the federal leader of the New Democratic party (NDP), demanded on Tuesday that Ottawa address the contract and further restrict Canadian corporations from doing business with ICE, after Canada’s National Observer reported on the deal. Continue reading...

events.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
events.description: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common. The central Japan cities of Gujo and Tajimi in Gifu prefecture, along with Toyota in Aichi prefecture, all hit 40C. Gujo’s 40C and Toyota’s 40.1C were all-time highs recorded at their respective weather stations. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat

events.title: Netflix viewers the world over are binge-watching South African series 'The Polygamist.' Here's why

events.title: Giorgia Meloni: The face of Europe's far right?
events.description: Giorgia Meloni made history in 2022 when she became Italy's first female prime minister. She quickly signalled her ideological affinity with Donald Trump and positioned herself as a bridge between the MAGA movement in the United States and Europe's radical right. She was also the only European leader invited to attend Trump's second inauguration in 2025. Renaud Lefort examines how the relationship between Trump and Meloni has evolved since then, alongside Meloni's changing role within the European Union. While she has sought to strengthen Italy's influence in Brussels, critics argue that she has moderated some of her more hardline positions in order to remain politically influential. The report features an interview with Gianfranco Fini, founder of the post-fascist National Alliance, where Meloni began her political career more than two decades ago. Historian and Italian politics specialist Marc Lazar also assesses Meloni's first three years in office, examining what he describes as authoritarian tendencies in her approach to governing.

events.title: Giorgia Meloni's Italy: An authoritarian drift? (Part 2)
events.description: In this edition of Europe Rendezvous, we travel to Italy, one of the European Union's founding members, to examine how the country's democratic institutions have come under increasing pressure. It may be the season of la dolce vita, but life has been far from sweet for migrants, women seeking abortions in parts of Italy, and journalists facing growing political pressure on the media. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni leads a coalition of conservatives and the far right, and as the 2027 elections approach, her government has been accused of seeking greater influence over the judiciary, the media and the electoral system.

events.title: Total solar eclipse books out hotels two years in advance in New Zealand city
events.description: Dunedin will be the focal point, having last experienced such an eclipse in 1163 – before any humans reached New Zealand Hotels are booking out two years in advance in the New Zealand city of Dunedin as it prepares for the region’s first total solar eclipse in more than 850 years. At 4.17pm on 22 July 2028, the moon will pass in front of the sun, casting a roughly 100km-wide shadowy band across parts of southern New Zealand. Everywhere within that band – from tourist hotspot Queenstown in the west to Dunedin city in the east – will be plunged into daytime darkness for 2 minutes and 51 seconds. Continue reading...

events.title: Meloni's Italy: Fortress Europe?
events.description: Migration was at the heart of Giorgia Meloni's successful 2022 election campaign. Promising a tougher approach to irregular migration helped propel her to power, but what has her government achieved since then? One of its flagship policies was the creation of a controversial migrant processing centre in Albania. Yet the plan has repeatedly run into legal obstacles in Italy, raising questions about whether it can deliver on its promises. At the same time, Meloni has had to balance her hardline rhetoric with economic realities. Faced with labour shortages and pressure from businesses, her government has authorised around half a million additional work visas for the 2026–2029 period. Has Meloni's migration policy proved more pragmatic than her supporters expected? Whatever the answer, it has created political space on her right. One of the most prominent challengers is former army general Roberto Vannacci, who advocates an even tougher approach to immigration and openly calls for the "remigration" of migrants. Luke Brown reports.

events.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
events.description: This is it.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: U.S. allies hit with new tariffs object to Trump's forced labor claims
events.description: Australia and the European Union were among U.S. trade partners who criticized the tariffs.

events.title: Three years under military rule: Niger still seeks stability
events.description: The military government in Niger had promised social, security and economic improvements when it usurped power in 2023. Three years down the line, there are mixed reviews on its performance.

events.title: Highs and lows as the Bayreuth Festival Turns 150
events.description: Bayreuth Festival's 150th anniversary is clouded by controversy surrounding German-Jewish speaker Michel Friedman. Yet it offers an opportunity to celebrate, including with an AI-enhanced Ring cycle.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Highs and lows as the Bayreuth Festival Turns 150

events.title: Germany news: Top court rules out mass admission revocations
events.description: An Afghan woman has won victory over Germany's Interior Ministry after it cancelled her admission. Elsewhere, consumer confidence and VW profits are down, while violent extremist crime is up. Follow DW for the latest.

events.title: Turkey: What chances does Ozel's new opposition party have?
events.description: After being removed as head of Turkey's main opposition CHP in May, Ozgur Ozel has announced the formation of a new party. Dozens of lawmakers have followed him, but will he be able to challenge President Erdogan?

events.title: Ousted Turkish opposition leader forms second-largest party
events.description: With 90 lawmakers defecting alongside him, Ozgur Ozel's New Party has automatically become the second-largest group in Turkey's parliament. He leaves behind deep devisions and judicial challenges against the CHP.

events.title: How vanilla's history is not so … 'vanilla'
events.description: Long before "vanilla" meant bland, the bean's fandom spanned Mesoamerican elites to European courts. But it owes its ubiquity to a 12-year-old, enslaved boy.

events.title: Germany: Friedrich Merz announces Cabinet reshuffle
events.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has announced a Cabinet reshuffle in the wake of the resignation of conservative heavyweight Jens Spahn over a surrogacy controversy. More changes are expected in the coming days.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany: Friedrich Merz announces Cabinet reshuffle

events.title: 'Extremely significant' car bomb intercepted near Irish border
events.description: An "extremely significant" car bomb has been intercepted by Irish police near the border with Northern Ireland.
events.ticker_label: 💥 'Extremely significant' car bomb intercepted near Irish border

events.title: US imposes new tariffs on 60 countries - including UK
events.description: The Trump administration has imposed new tariffs of at least 10% on imports from 60 countries over claims they failed to prevent forced labour.

events.title: TikTok charged with breaching EU rules on online safety for children
events.description: TikTok has been charged with breaching EU online content rules designed to protect children from predators or cyberbullying.

events.title: Poland's biggest opposition party faces split over tensions about its direction
events.description: Poland's main opposition party, Law and Justice, faces a split in a disagreement over its future direction

events.title: Bangladesh's president, once a close ally of ousted premier Sheikh Hasina, resigns
events.description: Bangladesh’s President Mohammed Shahabuddin has resigned halfway through his term, citing medical reasons

events.title: Singer Chris Brown pleads guilty to affray over an altercation at a London nightclub
events.description: Singer Chris Brown has pleaded guilty in a London court to affray over an altercation at a London nightclub

events.title: South Africa halts impeachment against President Cyril Ramaphosa over stolen cash inquiry
events.description: A South African court has ordered a halt to impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa

events.title: China slaps export controls on 14 EU entities for Russia-related sanctions
events.description: China says it's adding 14 European entities to an export control list in retaliation for the European Union penalizing 14 Chinese enterprises as part of its latest round of sanctions against Russia

events.title: Deposed Turkish opposition leader forms 'New Party' to challenge Erdogan
events.description: Ozgur Ozel, the deposed leader of Turkey's main opposition party, has formed a new political party to challenge President Erdogan

events.title: German leader Merz reshuffles top posts as he tries to counter far-right advance
events.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has reshuffled top posts as he tries to boost his unpopular government
events.ticker_label: 💥 German leader Merz reshuffles top posts as he tries to counter far-right advance

events.title: Purported Nancy Guthrie note includes unusual demand that breaks basic ransom rule: retired FBI negotiator
events.description: A retired FBI negotiator says the first ransom note in the Nancy Guthrie Tucson kidnapping case is inconsistent with a typical ransom motive.

events.title: Amazon Teacher of the Year winner accused of preying on 14-year-old student
events.description: Tymond Tran, a 2022 Amazon Future Engineer Teacher of the Year recipient, faces rape and indecent assault charges involving a 14-year-old student.

events.title: Good Samaritan in Spider-Man costume helps man in wheelchair cross busy intersection in viral moment
events.description: Video from Jonesboro, Arkansas, traffic cameras captured a driver in a Spider-Man costume helping a person in a wheelchair cross an intersection.

events.title: Trump returns to White House Correspondents’ Dinner after alleged assassination attempt and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Accused killer's alleged disguises unravel as sheriff releases new clues in month-long manhunt
events.description: Walton County Sheriff's Office reveals distinctive tattoos on fugitive Isabelle Johnson as the monthlong manhunt for the murder suspect continues.

events.title: ICE arrests Southwest Airlines flight attendant in Nashville for allegedly overstaying visa since 2021
events.description: Lorenzo Thompson, a Jamaican citizen and Southwest Airlines flight attendant, was arrested by ICE in Nashville after allegedly overstaying his visa.

events.title: Alex Murdaugh ranks among South Carolina’s worst tax deadbeats while awaiting new murder trial
events.description: South Carolina revenue records list Alex Murdaugh's $1.6 million tax debt as his lawyers push to test unknown male DNA found under Maggie's fingernails.

events.title: Florida woman on the run and wanted for murder relied on network of alleged accomplices to evade capture: Cops
events.description: Isabelle Rose Johnson allegedly uses disguises and aliases to evade arrest after Jason Christopher Coulthart's remains were found in Freeport, Florida.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Florida woman on the run and wanted for murder relied on network of alleged accomplices to evade capture: Cops

events.title: $290M cocaine haul found ‘skillfully concealed’ in massive banana shipment
events.description: Italian officials said the 1,700-pound haul of cocaine, hidden among banana pallets, could have generated roughly $290 million on the street.

events.title: Sinaloa Cartel boss 'El Mayo' sentenced to life, ordered to pay $15B in drug trafficking profits
events.description: Sinaloa Cartel co-founder Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada Garcia received a life sentence and was ordered to forfeit $15 billion in drug trafficking profits.

events.title: Andy Burnham takes over as UK prime minister, calls for 'new political model'
events.description: Andy Burnham enters Downing Street vowing to shift power away from London, but his past criticism of Trump could create early friction with Washington.
